mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators who need to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This repository contains the necessary charts for installation.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are essential for defining the custom resources that Cluster API will manage.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> This command installs the required CRDs for Cluster API.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components into the specified namespace.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

```

> Ensure you have the correct namespace and version.

steps.title: Create a Cluster API custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource that defines the desired state of your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProvider
    name: my-infrastructure
EOF

```

> Replace 'MyInfrastructureProvider' with your specific infrastructure provider.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProvider
    name: my-infrastructure
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Cluster API components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Look for pods with names starting with 'cluster-api' to ensure they are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API
uninstall.description:
This step removes Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this will lead to data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> This command removes the Cluster API installation.
> This command deletes the CRDs associated with Cluster API.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your cluster state. This step includes draining nodes if necessary, upgrading the components, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Drains all nodes in the cluster.
> This command upgrades the Cluster API components.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get clusters -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD is not installed or not recognized.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: infrastructure provider not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified infrastructure provider is not installed or configured.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the correct infrastructure provider is installed and referenced in your Cluster custom resource.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in kube-system are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This typically indicates a misconfiguration in the Cluster API components.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the failing pods using: kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system and correct any configuration issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the API server is not reachable or is overloaded.

**Fix:**
Check the health of your Kubernetes cluster and ensure that the API server is running and accessible.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for multi-cluster management, added new infrastructure providers.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for older Kubernetes versions below 1.26.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to update your custom resources to match the new API versions.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get clusters' showing your cluster with a status of 'Ready'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProvider
    name: my-infrastructure
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
